title: 10 best herbal creams for tooth decay

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Pot marigold cream contains antimicrobial compounds that inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, which can cause tooth decay by breaking down enamel and producing acid.

remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds, found in Calendula officinalis, have antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria associated with tooth decay, such as Streptococcus mutans.

remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Calendula officinalis are used due to their high content of flavonoids and triterpenoids, which exhibit antimicrobial properties.

Seeds: The seeds of Calendula officinalis are used due to their rich content of fatty acids and saponins, which have antimicrobial properties that help in preventing oral infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 flowers of the medicinal plant for use in the cream at the peak of bloom.
Infuse the flowers in 2 cups of oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the oil and discard the solids then add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Melt the mixture over low heat stirring constantly until the beeswax and shea butter are fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and set completely before use.

remedy_recipe: Infuse dried melissa flowers in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil 1:5 for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discard solids.
Weigh out beeswax 20% and shea butter 10% of total weight and melt in a double boiler.
Add the melissa infused oil to the melted wax and butter mixture then stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into clean glass jars and allow to cool and harden for 30 minutes to an hour.

Barks: Contain tannins that help to reduce plaque and prevent oral infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh berberis aquifolium leaves in the morning after dew has dried off and ensure they are free of any pesticides or contaminants.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours or until they become crispy and brittle.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes to ensure uniform texture.
Mix 20 grams of the powdered berberis aquifolium with 100 grams of coconut oil in a glass jar and stir well for 10 minutes until it forms a smooth paste.
Allow the mixture to infuse at room temperature for 2 hours before straining it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with tooth decay because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce plaque buildup and prevent the growth of bacteria in the mouth. The cream's natural properties also aid in neutralizing acid production, which can lead to tooth erosion and cavities. Additionally, dandelion extracts have been shown to stimulate saliva production, helping to remineralize teeth and strengthen enamel, ultimately reducing the risk of tooth decay and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Inulin: Inulin, a prebiotic fiber found in dandelion roots, helps to remineralize teeth and prevent decay by feeding beneficial bacteria in the mouth, which produces acids that strengthen tooth enamel.

Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpenoid saponin in dandelion roots, has been shown to exhibit antibacterial properties, inhibiting the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a bacteria commonly associated with tooth decay.

Kaempferol: Kaempferol, a flavonoid present in dandelion flowers and leaves, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and prevent the damage caused by free radicals in the mouth, which can contribute to tooth decay.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have been traditionally used to make natural creams for tooth decay due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce gum inflammation and prevent further damage.
Rhyzomes: Dandelion rhyzomes contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps to regulate the growth of beneficial oral bacteria, promoting healthy teeth and gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves for this recipe.
Dry the taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves using a low-temperature oven for 2 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the ground taraxacum officinale with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler at medium heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer to a glass jar for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with tooth decay because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The aloe vera gel in these creams has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gums, which can help prevent the onset of tooth decay. Additionally, aloe's antimicrobial compounds work to eliminate bacteria that can cause cavities and gum disease, promoting a healthy mouth environment. By using an herbal aloe cream regularly, individuals can support their oral health and reduce their risk of tooth decay.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Aloe vera's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to tooth decay and cavities.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and pain associated with tooth decay, making it a natural remedy for toothaches.
Gel-like consistency: The gel-like consistency of aloe vera helps to soothe and protect the gums, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by acid erosion from tooth decay.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm gum inflammation, making them an effective addition to tooth decay creams.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the aloe plant possess antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for tooth decay, helping to prevent further damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves for extraction of gel containing bioactive compounds.
Extract the gel from 3-4 aloe vera leaves using cold water and blending technique.
Mix 20 grams of extracted aloe vera gel with 10 milliliters of coconut oil and beeswax.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to facilitate emulsification.
Allow the cooled cream to set at room temperature for at least 2 hours before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with tooth decay because they contain thujone, a natural compound that has potent antibacterial properties. When applied topically to the affected area, wormwood cream can help reduce inflammation and combat the growth of bacteria that cause cavities and tooth decay. Additionally, wormwood's antiseptic properties help to prevent further infection and promote healing, making it an effective natural remedy for combating tooth decay.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood's antimicrobial compounds, such as thujone and sesquiterpenes, help combat bacteria that contribute to tooth decay by inhibiting their growth and preventing them from producing acid.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, making it an effective natural remedy for reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with tooth decay.

Astringent properties: Wormwood's astringent properties help to reduce bleeding, shrink swollen gums, and dry out pus-filled pimples or abscesses caused by tooth decay, promoting healing and reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bitter compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and fight against bacteria that cause tooth decay.
Barks: Contain tannins, which have antimicrobial properties that can prevent bacterial growth and promote healthy teeth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of dried artemisia absinthium leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered artemisia absinthium with 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating it at low heat for 30 minutes to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and allowing it to cool for 15 minutes.
Mix the cooled infused oil with 20g of beeswax, 10ml of sweet almond oil, and 5ml of peppermint essential oil in a heat-resistant bowl, heating it over low heat until melted and smooth.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars, sealing them tightly and storing them at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerating for longer storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for tooth decay are natural topical remedies that help prevent and treat tooth decay by providing antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. These creams typically contain ingredients such as aloe vera, neem, and tea tree oil, which have been shown to reduce plaque and gum inflammation. Examples of herbal creams that help with tooth decay include Neem Oil Tooth Cream and Aloe Vera Gel. By using these creams, individuals can improve their oral health, reduce pain and discomfort, and maintain a healthy, beautiful smile.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to tooth decay that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions such as acne, eczema, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. These natural remedies can also treat wounds and ulcers, providing an alternative to conventional treatments for these common skin issues.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with tooth decay is a blend of Neem, Clove, and Aloe vera. Neem oil's antimicrobial properties fight bacteria causing decay, while Clove oil's analgesic properties ease pain and inflammation. Aloe vera's soothing properties calm gums and promote healing. Apply a small amount of each cream separately, then mix them together to create a paste. Brush your teeth gently with the paste for 2-3 minutes, twice a day, to help prevent and heal tooth decay naturally. Regular use may also reduce plaque and gum inflammation.
